---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, splash.scss]
    embed-resources: true
    slide-number: false
    controls: false
    progress: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    center: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Rubik:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
---

##  {.splash .nostretch}

![](images/a2ai-logo-color.png){.splash-logo fig-align="center"}

::: {.splash-topic}
**dvs** &nbsp;·&nbsp; _pronounced "devious"_
:::

::: {.splash-name}
Mossa Merhi Reimert
:::

::: {.splash-title}
Sr. Scientific Software Engineer
:::

## What is dvs?

**dvs** is a data version control system for R projects.

It stores large data files in a content-addressed **storage directory** — separate from your git repo — and tracks them through small, text-based **meta files** that live alongside your code.

::: {.fragment}
git tracks code · dvs tracks data
:::

## Why not just git?

| | git | dvs |
|---|---|---|
| Source code | ✅ | — |
| Config files | ✅ | — |
| Data files (MBs–GBs) | ❌ slow / bloats history | ✅ |
| Reproducible restores | partial | ✅ content-addressed |
| Team sharing | via remote | via shared mount |

## Agenda

| | Vignette |
|---|---|
| 1 | **intro** — R API: init, add, status, get |
| 2 | **intro-cli** — same operations from the terminal |
| 3 | **intro-internals** — `.dvs/` layout, blobs, audit log |
| 4 | **lifecycle** — modify, unsynced, dry-run, integrity failures |
| 5 | **collab** — sharing data across projects |
| 6 | **config** — dvs.toml, compression, threads |
| 7 | **audit** — querying the audit log |

## How to read these

**R API** (`dvs_*` functions) and **CLI** (`dvs` binary) are equivalent — choose whichever fits your workflow.

```r
dvs_add("data/file.csv", message = "v1")
```

```bash
dvs add data/file.csv -m "v1"
```

::: {.fragment}
**storage** = the blob directory (shared, not in git)

**project** = your R project (meta files committed, data files gitignored)
:::
